Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated serum creatinine). Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal tubular dysfunction or nephrotic syndrome. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and correlation with clinical findings for possible renal pathology and to clarify the cause of abnormal urine findings.
